tribe_description: A tribe of pragmatic value gamers who judge products through a gaming and entertainment lens. They generally prioritize practical results, value for money.
tribe_definition: A tribe of pragmatic value gamers who judge products through a gaming and entertainment lens. They generally prioritize practical results, value for money.
population_definition: Gamers who evaluate video games, consoles, accessories, and entertainment products.

inherent_behavioral_traits.text: Hands-on testers who compare to previous gear
Consumer-advocate mindset, warning others of pitfalls
Focus on functional utility over flashy features

main.text: Securing the best performance per dollar
Avoiding technical hassles, crashes or incompatibility
Sharing troubleshooting knowledge with the community
Maximizing comfort and usability during extended play
Sidestepping predatory ads, DRM, or upsells

friction_points: Frequent bugs, lag, or connection drop-outs
Poor build quality (e.g., flimsy D-pads, weak mics)
Compatibility failures with specific systems or titles
Excessive ads, paywalls, or restrictive DRM

implicit_goals.text: Avoid wasting money on sub-par or incompatible products
Build a dependable, cross-platform gaming setup
Empower peers with concise, actionable purchase advice

member_user_characteristics.characteristic_summary: This user is value-conscious and technically minded, focusing on gameplay mechanics, balance, and software stability rather than superficial aspects like graphics. Their reviews are driven by a desire to protect fellow consumers from paying full price for products they deem unfinished or inferior to previous versions, offering detailed, comparative critiques that highlight practical flaws and quality-assurance issues.

This user is a technically savvy, quality-focused gamer who writes reviews to guide fellow gamers toward the best overall experience. Their evaluations center on performance (smoothness, crashes, ads, permissions), audiovisual fidelity, interface design, and value, showing a desire to help others avoid frustrations and appreciate well-crafted, immersive gameplay. Their detailed, comparative comments reveal an analytical mindset driven by sharing practical, device-specific insights rather than mere opinion.

category_characteristics.Video Games: This user is a passionate JRPG enthusiast who values narrative depth, believable characters, and high-quality voice acting, using prior franchise entries as benchmarks. They write upbeat, comparative reviews to share enthusiasm with fellow fans while also noting practical considerations like graphics performance and purchase price, aiming to reassure others of both the artistic merits and value-for-money of a title.

user_history_reviews.review_text: I got this for the iPad and it's an amazing fun game with gorgeous graphics, sound and intuitive game play. The controls are precise as well. Get it free while you can and this is one game I wouldn't mind paying full price. It's that good.

EDIT: I'm changing my review back to 5 stars. Another reviewer mentioned turning off 3D audio for those who are getting pops and static instead of sound.

I'm glad to see it made it to the Amazon App Store. I already purchased it for my iPad. It's challenging but fun and very immersive. You are basically landing the Space Shuttle on its final approach and you can customize weather and landing conditions.

I'm running it on my first gen Kindle Fire HD and it's ultra smooth. I do like the iOS interface a little better as the menus are laid out better.

That said you'll spend hours trying to get a safe or good landing. I still haven't pulled off a perfect landing but I'm trying.

I thought I'd give this app a try. I was hoping it was similar to Flight Control. The game kept crashing because it was trying to load ads. On the third try the game finally loaded and the screen tried to sell me another app and took me out of the game and into the app store.

On the Kindle Fire HD the lower left of the screen is taken up by a Kohler faucet ad. Lot's of permissions too. Don't waste the download.
I love the idea and the different spin on cut the rope and where's my water. The music is catchy and the graphics are simple and fit the game quite well.

member_user_characteristics.characteristic_summary: This user is a pragmatic hobbyist who rigorously assesses products for build quality, functional effectiveness, comfort, and value-for-money, especially in relation to their VR gaming and outdoor activities. Their reviews are vivid, detailed, and emotionally expressive, aiming to warn others against overpriced or poorly made items while praising gear that solves practical problems with ease of use and thoughtful design. Overall, they write as a consumer advocate determined to help peers avoid frustration and make smart, utility-driven purchases.

user_history_reviews.review_text: Oh my god, what a disappointment.  These are so expensive and sound like absolute junk.  I got them to use with my Oculus Quest 2 after seeing a video that recommended them.  But after plugging them in I found that the sound quality was far inferior to just using the stock, built in speakers within the Quest.  Huge disappointment!!!!

I recommend these grips highly!  They are awesome!  Previously I had grips from Nexigo, and they were fine, but incredibly, incredibly difficult to put on, and every time a battery needed to be replaced I had to take them all the way apart again.  Ugh!  But these ones are super easy to install, and have a built in battery access door!

This is absolute junk.  It is cheaply made and it is a sin to sell this for any price.  The headset jack is so loosely installed it bends in every direction, so the keyboard never mounts firmly. The slightest touch of it with your fingertips makes it unplug and fall off the controller.  Also the electronics are defective and several keys stopped working in less than a week of use. Avoid buying this!

First of all, I waited 45 days for this to arrive.  Winter is almost over.  But second, I am very, very disappointed in these.  They are horribly uncomfortable.  I mean like "GET THEM OFF ME NOW, I WANT TO JUMP OUT OF MY SKIN WITH DISCOMFORT" kind of uncomfortable.  The materials are a terrible choice.  And the fit is terrible.  I am a man, 5' 9" average build and weight, and ordered 3XL to be safe, but these are so tight I had trouble squeezing into them.  3XL in China is apparently A LOT smaller than 3XL in the USA.  They don't come with power banks, and you need separate power banks for the shirt and pants.  And the heat is very inconsistent.  The listing photos make it look like there are several heating coils running all over them, but in the shirt I can only feel heat on my lower back, on the right side.  And on the pants, I can only feel heat on the right knee.  That's on the "High" heat setting.  I was unable to feel any heat on the lower settings.  These are way too expensive as well.  Save your money.  In retrospect, I wish I had not bought them at all.

For the price, I am very pleased with this vest.  It does feel a bit flimsy and cheap, but after all, it is.  Still I love it.  I was looking for a way to give me more pockets for carrying various batteries and items I need when I go hiking and doing photography, so that I wouldn't have to keep pulling off my backpack for every little thing (pulling off a backpack and putting it back on can be a bit of a chore for an old fart like me).  This vest does the trick.  Lots of wonderful pockets!

These are awesome!  I had no idea such a thing existed.  I've been hiking in the outdoors this winter in New England, and although I have hiking boots, I was still slipping a lot.  You never know what's under the snow sometimes, it could be wet leaves, or sand, or ice, or unstable or oddly shaped rocks.  It's a gamble with every step you take.  But these things, which are easy and quick to install and remove, give you an immense amount of traction.  Of course, you don't want to wear them indoors, or for that matter, when walking on any dry surfaces.  But for hiking in the winter they are phenomenal.

member_user_characteristics.characteristic_summary: This user is a technically inclined gamer who prioritizes build quality, brand reliability, and practical functionality (e.g., compatibility, cord length, rechargeable power) while still noting price value. Their reviews are concise, checklist-oriented endorsements meant to guide like-minded enthusiasts toward trustworthy, high-performing gear and to share confidence in brands that have proven consistent for them.

Their reviews use a concise, checklist style to confirm key functional criteria and to quickly reassure fellow gamers that the product will work, feel durable, and be worth the money.

user_history_reviews.review_text:
This keyboard is very nice. It has a good feel to it and a nice height for extended typing sessions. The backlight is only one color, but it is an easy-on-the-eyes purple with 2 brightness levels (regular and a little brighter) and an off option which is easily switchable with a brightness adjuster button near the top of the keyboard.

Just to add, any Logitech item I've ever purchased has always been awesome with great build quality. I wouldn't hesitate to purchase this keyboard or any other Logitech item for that matter.

They write balanced, detail-rich reviews that outline pros, cons, and practical tips so that others can set realistic expectations and improve their own experience—whether mastering a tactical shooter or choosing reliable house shoes. Their reviews are detailed tutorials meant to set realistic expectations—covering learning curves, technical requirements, and community dynamics—so that like-minded players can make informed decisions and improve together. They are motivated by continuous skill mastery and a desire to foster cooperative, well-prepared player groups.

user_history_reviews.review_text: These fit kinda tight at first, but they broken in quickly.  They are easy to slip on and stay on well.  When walking they stay in place and don't seem to flip flop like other similar house shoes.  They have good non-skid rubber soles for outside.  Overall, they were a good gift and fit exactly as expected.

user_history_reviews.review_text: A must have for Colts fans with a PS4. I’m loving looking at my console all shiny with the blue and white draped on it. Easy to apply, me and my seven year old applied in less than ten minutes.
A little long for my heigth (5'9") but very cool looking, good color, and isnt uncomfortable at all. Would recommend for misbehaving

I’m very pleased with the jacket. Fits exceptionally well. Almost a tailored fit. It’s not too heavy for the southern winters but it is leather so warm enough. It had a strong leather smell but went away. It also had fold lines that went away with some saddle soap and elbow grease. I recommend this jacket.

I've bought $10 headsets that have a better mic than the TE Kraken. You have to put the mic up to 100% to be able to even be heard just a tiny bit. However, the sound works great. :)
I think it fit nicely for my Wreck It Ralph disney bound.

user_history_reviews.review_text: I love the retractable microphone and the ear cuffs are surprisingly comfy. The cord is of decent length and the sound is decent enough for stuff like COD or watching Netflix. My only gripe is the weird bungee headband thing instead of being able to slide up or down to adjust the earmuffs.
I like the fact it includes multiple USB ports and plugs. Frees up some space on my desk. Wish it came with some cables but I had some lying around

Cheap headband but you get what you pay for. I’m replacing some turtle beaches that the audio died in one ear as seems to be a frequent problem with the z11’s at least in my experience. These were cheap, and in my favorite color so I’m pleased. Will see how long they last.
Did absolutely nothing to prevent my PS4 from over heating. Loud and annoying and the fans are blowing the wrong way to get proper airflow.
Too small and took over a week to arrive
Very comfortable, looks much more expensive than it is. The zippers are cheap and its a bit smaller around the waist length than I was expecting but other than that so far so good.

user_history_reviews.review_text: dumb kids app

Bought this for the xbox 1 in particular and it works great. Set up was fairly easy with a youtube search. Microsoft doesn't make real easy for 3rd party stuff but the keyboard is bright and very durable and the mouse feels good and seems tough and accurate. Great deal at $25

Works perfect with no compatibilty problems. Slim console immediately recognizes it. Some games require a harddrive to be installed before playing (titanfall, battlefield 4) so this a cheap way around that. Install is easy. Pop the little hatch on the bottom vent, insert drive til it clicks, pop vent cover back on, done!

user_history_reviews.review_text: TurboTax does it all.  I do wonder whether with the Standard Deduction such sophisticated software is necessary, though.  Who knows...

The single person version of Office 365 has a lot in it for a good price.  OneNote, Word, Excel, Publisher, Powerpoint, Outlook, Skype, and 1TB of OneDrive.  Anyone still use an app like Outlook?  Why?

Symantec makes an excellent product. I will probably keep it going.  But they wanted $100 to renew for another year which is way too much.  So I bought this extension at Amazon, instead.  This does the same exact things as the auto renewal.  But beware. Do wait until the last day of your current subscription, otherwise the start date may be today for you. Their customer support will fix this, but it's an unnecessary headache.

These chargers work as they should and are very convenient.  They arrived from Amazon yesterday.  I put both my XBox SX controllers on them.  One controller got a green light after about 30 minutes.  The other took overnight I can't explain that.  The indicator lights are bright. I may partially cover them with electrical tape.

AG2ZJ65OIEP3RSTNW7J4NL2LL6JA.review_text: Something is not right with this game and needs more QA refinement. Graphics are good but gameplay mechanics are far worse than 2K19 especially in 6 or 8 person matches. Hit detection is abysmal, attacks float through AI but AI always connects, AI spams infinite reversals. 1/2 roster is outdated due to budget cuts or 2021 attire. Also, random blue screen bug error during matches. Wait for a discount - definitely not worth full price.

predicted_themes: Hardware Build Quality Comfort and Connectivity

AF2GITXLXFV6GQQVWASFQSRWGN4A.product_description: It's time to start high school at Shujin Academy in Persona 5, so be prepared for all the danger and drama of the non-conformist teenage life with the PlayStation 4 exclusive “Take Your Heart” Premium Edition. Rebelling against the man requires the right tools and style, and this premium edition has what the disaffected youth needs to do it. The game disc will be encased in a SteelBook featuring a bold design inspired by the game's theme of teenage rebellion. Also included is a CD featuring a selection of the game's unforgettably catchy acid jazz soundtrack from legendary composer Shoji Meguro. Plus there is a hardcover art book full of concept sketches, character art, and more by character designer Shigenori Soejima. Exclusive to this set is a 4-inch plush of Morgana the cat/team member/mascot. Also exclusive is a Japanese-style school bag emblazoned with the Shujin Academy crest. All of these items come in a large collector's edition box that will simply look like a video game to nosey authority figures. Persona 5 is a game about the internal and external conflicts of a group of troubled high school students - the protagonist and a collection of compatriots he meets in the game's story - who live dual lives as Phantom Thieves. They have the typically ordinary day-to-day of a Tokyo high schooler - attending class, after school activities and part-time jobs. But they also undertake fantastical adventures by using otherworldly powers to enter the hearts of people. Their power comes from the Persona, the Jungian concept of the “self”. The game's heroes realize that society forces people to wear masks to protect their inner vulnerabilities, and by literally ripping off their protective masks and confronting their inner selves do the heroes awaken their inner power, and use it to strive to help those in need. Ultimately, the group of Phantom Thieves seek to change their day-to-day world to match their perception and see through the masks modern-day society wears.
AF2GITXLXFV6GQQVWASFQSRWGN4A.review_text: I love this series although my ultimate favorite to this day is still Persona 3 because of the script, the cast of believable characters and the voice acting. Persona 5 takes that up a notch and then some. Well worth your time if you are remotely interested in SJRPG.

AH7GM7OQ3X663PMTPLFW3U35Z4VA.product_description: Yaktrax Diamond Grip All-Surface Traction Cleats provide superior traction for walking in all winter conditions, from boilerplate ice to hard-packed snow and everything in between. Diamond Grips unique design makes them ideal for users who frequently transition between surfaces like rock, gravel, dirt, and concrete. Sturdy rubber sling bands hold Diamond Grips firmly in place over boots or shoes, yet they remain elastic even in sub-zero temperatures so that they are easy to put on and take off regardless the weather. Diamond Grip’s patented Diamond Beads are made from case-hardened steel alloy with hundreds of edges that grip in all directions. The beads swivel independently preventing build-up of snow and ice. Yaktrax Diamond Grip All-Surface Traction Cleats are built to last with aircraft-grade steel cables, case-hardened steel alloy Diamond Beads, Zinc coated steel grommets, and durable rubber sling bands. Yaktrax Diamond Grip All-Surface Cleats come in five sizes to fit most shoe and boot sizes. (Yaktrax recommends adding one shoe size for regular boots and two shoe sizes for insulated boots): Small (fits shoe size W5-7, M5-6); Medium (fits shoe size W7.5-10, M6.5-9); Large (fits shoe size W10.5+, M9.5-12.5); X-Large (fits shoe size M 13+); 2XL (fits shoe size M13+ insulated). 90-day manufacturer’s warranty.
AH7GM7OQ3X663PMTPLFW3U35Z4VA.review_text: These are awesome!  I had no idea such a thing existed.  I've been hiking in the outdoors this winter in New England, and although I have hiking boots, I was still slipping a lot.  You never know what's under the snow sometimes, it could be wet leaves, or sand, or ice, or unstable or oddly shaped rocks.  It's a gamble with every step you take.  But these things, which are easy and quick to install and remove, give you an immense amount of traction.  Of course, you don't want to wear them indoors, or for that matter, when walking on any dry surfaces.  But for hiking in the winter they are phenomenal.

AFN222ZYTTHXQ7NAG4AEIRZWRIIA.product_description: Av-s Video Composite Cable for Nintendo 64 N64, Gamecube, & Super Nintendo Snes
AFN222ZYTTHXQ7NAG4AEIRZWRIIA.review_text: This product doesn't feel cheap or chintzy and it works. AFQPRFHOBZZ2PAXE7PBEFCXRUAMA.product_description: Get the BEST of both worlds with the Capcom themed dual Link Controller by Retro-Bit. Compatible with original Genesis Consoles or your PC and Mac, this unique dual Link cable gives you a new way to experience your favorite games!
AFQPRFHOBZZ2PAXE7PBEFCXRUAMA.review_text: Not much else to say besides what is in the review title. If you're looking for another controller for your Sega Genesis Mini, do NOT buy this; it doesn't work with the Mini. It DOES, however, work great on both PC and original Sega Genesis consoles, as well as the vast majority of Sega Genesis "clone" consoles that have a 9-pin controller] port. For those wondering, YES, it does have a Mode button, and it's positioned in exactly the same place as it is on the original Sega Genesis 6-button controllers (where the "R" button) would be on a modern controller. I got it for cheap while it was on sale for like $15, so I'm happy with it, but I don't think I would've gone as high as $20 for it, because, again, it does NOT work with the Genesis Mini, but the d-pad works and feels great, as do the buttons, so it's a well-built piece of hardware. Just a shame it doesn't work on the Mini...

AFBXZ7P7OZTPJKEHSTP5X5WLUSBQ.product_description: Squad up and breach in to explosive 5v5 PVP action. Tom Clancy's Rainbow Six Siege features a huge roster of specialized operators, each with game-changing gadgets to help you lead your team to victory.
AFBXZ7P7OZTPJKEHSTP5X5WLUSBQ.review_text:
I have to admit that I'm a Battlefield person that used to play mostly COD. Over the last few months, I've really noticed the popularity of competitive Rainbow 6 Siege online. The curiosity alone made me interested. This game has the mostly standard FPS layout with more emphasis on stealth & tactics. If you are used to COD run & gun - then this will take a bit of getting used to. The "Situations" are necessary before multiplayer & you'll be glad you got this practice before hand. These are designed specifically for showing you the methods of slow, steady, smart situational awareness. This gives you time to practice alternative entries, tactics & gadgets - not just shooting & clearing.

The main issue I had with the game is that straight out the box there was a 20gb update. Personally, I have several games I play regularly & had to delete one of them to make room on my PS4 hard drive for R6S. That's the bad. The good is that I do plan on playing regularly for the time being to get better at different operators, so I won't miss the other game anytime soon. This particular gold edition does include exclusive operator access, just FYI.

To consider: this game does require time to develop your skills. I've put in very few hours in what would be needed to really excel. But, don't worry too much. The online ranking system puts people of similar rank in groups. My suggestion? Find friends that play this game & learn together. Working in groups is paramount to the objective & good communication with other players is also exceedingly necessary. If you find a few friends in the existing community or those that are wanting to play, then playing together regularly would be a big plus. This is similar to other games where "squads" or "clans" play together & know how to supplement each other's abilities.

I feel like putting in some time will benefit your experience greatly. This game can be anything from casual to competitive, with the only difference being your knowledge of the operators & practicing. I can admit, I need a lot more practice. But, I feel the benefit of improving my skills every game. Rainbow Six Siege looks like it's going to have a long lifespan as far as developer support & new content. So on that front, I wouldn't worry about it being a game with dead servers in 6 months. In fact, this being the Year 2 edition - I had read that UBI Soft has commented that they are aiming for 50-100 different operators eventually. This leaves the door open to a continually living, breathing & evolving online community for years to come. To me, this seems exciting to imagine.

Last thoughts. As you play the multiplayer, use a mic when possible & prepare to plan your game tactics as a group. Knowing the maps & being able to call out info about the location of the other team - is the key element you can provide your team even after your operator's death. The matches I had with those kinds of people were the most effective. Other matches where people just each ran different ways & played single - those games often resulted in one good player being the hero or were just disastrous. There is ample time to learn these things as an individual & a team - but just wanted to point it out. I do see myself playing this quite a bit & would recommend for anyone interested in a very different style of FPS.

4.5 stars

AH6H4LT6VTERZXYY6XFKNIEHPUWA.review_text: I bought these to replace my x12s and I have to say I was extremely dissatisfied. The volume is atrociously low. So low that it renders them useless. AENCFNSLMF7KWKR4VWHAQU3IJF4A.product_description: Product Description ÜberSoldier challenges players to take on the role of a genetically modified super-soldier brought back from beyond the grave, as he seeks retribution against the enemies that surround him. Join resistance fighters in the struggle to defeat Nazi occupation forces in dozens of action-packed missions that require quick, on-your-feet thinking and tactical prowess. Arm yourself with both tactical supernatural powers and a veritable arsenal of historically based weaponry. Amazon.com In Ubersoldier , you become an elite German soldier who was killed in an ambush by the French Resistance in 1944. Fortunately for you, your chances to serve the homeland aren't over, because the SS are running a program in which they resurrect the dead, turning them into fearless "Ubersoldiers" with paranormal powers. You wield a handy knife for gutting foes, as well a number of authentic WWII weapons. View larger . You even get to control a sub's torpedoes during one mission. View larger . The graphics for the weapons and environments are first class, with excellent detail and sharp textures. View larger . Unfortunately for the homeland, the dead obey the first person who speaks to them after their resurrection, which in your case means a German resistance operative who has freed you from the SS labs for her own ends. Now, rather than faithfully fighting for the Fatherland, you will try to undermine its efforts by putting an end to the entire Ubersoldier project. A straight-up first-person shooter, Ubersoldier focuses on the action. You wield a handy knife for gutting foes, as well a number of authentic World War II rifles, pistols, submachine guns, and grenades. You even get to control a submarine's torpedoes and anti-aircraft guns during one mission. The graphics for the weapon models and environments are first class, with excellent detail and sharp textures. One of your paranormal Ubersoldier powers is the ability to put up a bullet shield, which can stop bullets in their tracks Matrix -style. This ability is limited, however, as it is powered by a meter that gets re-charged by racking up head shots on your enemies in rapid succession. If your meter is powered enough, you can actually bounce bullets back at the enemies who shot at you. The graphics of the effect are spectacular, featuring a blue shimmer that actually suspends the individual bullets in midair. The campaign in Ubersoldier takes you across a slate of secret research facilities, bombed-out cities, military bases, and the aforementioned U-boat as you work for the Allies. Firefights are fast, frequent, and bloody, and the weapons feel as they have real force to them. The enemies in Ubersoldier are not exceedingly bright, but they're often cunning and extremely reactive. They will open up their machine guns the moment they see you and will heave grenades that will force you to clear out quickly -- that is, if you're not blown up first! In Ubersoldier , the ESRB-mature rating is for good reason. When you shoot a guy, the nearby walls and floor run red with blood, and if you hit him in the face a few times, you're screen will soon fill up with a reddened, leaking pulp. If an enemy has the misfortunate to end up in close proximity to a grenade blast, he will grab his head and stumble about, wailing in agony until you put him out of his misery. In fact, putting a few enemies temporarily out of service with a grenade, only to finish them off while they scream and suffer, is a macabre but effective technique. You will get to see some cool scripted events too, such as an enemy falling off a rooftop after getting shot, only to hit a power line in an explosion of sparks on the way down. The designers thoughtfully scattered just enough explosive fuel barrels and oxygen tanks around the levels to add to the mayhem, creating loads of smoke and fire, flying bodies and debris. Indoors, the game takes you from dank sewers to a plush auditorium, from a disgustingly urine-soaked bathroom to human experimentation laboratories, and everything in between. The lighting effects are well thought out and vary from location to location, and the walls and floors are liberally decorated with appropriate furnishings and odds and ends. The backgrounds are all animated, with air-duct fans that rotate, large pieces of machinery that chug and whirr, and control panels that blink and flash. Not for those who are squeamish or too easily startled, Ubersoldier boasts smooth battles that have plenty of visceral carnage, well-paced missions, and an overall strong sense of forward momentum that keeps you active without ever feeling harried.
AENCFNSLMF7KWKR4VWHAQU3IJF4A.review_text:
I like shooters, especially quirky ones. I'm even giving a lot of older games a try just to find a gem among the excrement.

This appears to be more the excrement kind than the gem kind. I was unable to run it under Windows 7, even in compatability mode for what it's advertised. After quite a lengthy install (did we really used to put up with three CDs slowly being copied onto a HD?), I got a non-Windows error message upon starting. The miniature type said something about software being missing. Windows' own error alerts showed me that I was missing numerous components of the StarForce drivers package.

So I ran the program with administrator privileges-- mistake! It said I had to reboot to run the application. (Huh?) I did... and got the text-based startup screen ("Windows is reading files") rather than the familiar graphical one. I was then taken to a "repair disk" screen and pretty much dropped a deuce right there. I had moved recently and hadn't hooked up my dedicated backup disk; fortunately I did have a restore point from the last Windows Update.

After rebooting, I checked the Web and found that StarFrce is an infamous copy-protection scheme, like SecuROM, which installs hidden drivers onto your system. Wikipedia links to a site purported to be a removal tool, but it's heavily Russian and casually translated into English, and doesn't seem to have the actual tool. A Google search came up with star-force.com/support/drivers, where I found SFREMOVE.EXE. (This site, like the Russian site, seems intent on forcing miniature text onto your browser. Perhaps a normal vision user could see it but I had to use magnification.)

Now, I'm hoping that this tool has cleaned my system out. Ubersoldier itself doesn't show up in Programs And Features for uninstallation, and the program (while it has some uninstall files) doesn't actually uninstall itself. I used the MacOS approach-- just trashing the folder and hoping it hasn't left any droppings on my HD. (Sorry this review is so poo-oriented, but that's how I'm feeling at the moment after flushing five bucks down the loo on this game. It's experiences like this that make piracy seem ethical.)

I guess you might be able to enjoy this game if you (1) had a PC with a 32-bit processor and a 2006-vintage graphics card, (2) were using Windows XP or 2000, (3) didn't mind clandestine software being forced onto your PC, and (D) have a backup of your system just in case. For my own part, I'll avoid games by CDV and keep looking for good old games-- Prey was very pleasing indeed, and installed without hassles, so good older games do exist. This just isn't one of them.

AHQLPVPLVA6NYE2NRGOP4XVEGKJQ.review_text:
Best fight pad in the world as of 2020.
Use for street fighter. Main Ken, Akuma and Kage.
Saw complaints about this pad not having a center axis.
Don’t know how that affects other games, but for me to pull off specials and combos, this pad is for real.
Better than hori fight commander for me. I have hori. Doing a medium punch hadoken 2 hit combo is easier on the raion pad.
Hori is great don’t get me wrong and cheaper. Bought hori for $24 GameStop April 2020.
For the price I will take hori, but for best functionality raion is the best.
You hear clicks on the the pad when performing moves and special.
Why don’t you buy this pad and try it out yourself you can return it back after 30 days if you don’t like it.

Patience and strategic thinking will pay off -- true Ninja mastery only comes with keen use of sound, sight and smell detection as you learn new moves and obtain new weapons. The guns (all primary auto look alike same thing for special and heavy) all look the same except for exotic weapons

This game was a disaster. The only reason i might get the upcoming DLC is to keep my stats; i.e if i really decide to.

Back to international superstar soccer Deluxe times

I always prefer PES over FIFA. Why? Gameplay period!
I played all PES starting from SNES 'ISS' - International Superstar Soccer.
I played up until 2013 when Konami was smoking some bad drug or something.
FIFA started to beat or catch up to KONAMI about 2012. Konami has messed up up until now. I even skipped PES 2014.
FIFA still has better presentation, online players / connection and licenses but Konami definitely better in gameplay and player animation.
The faces in Konami def better than EA also.
The best soccer game will arrive when Konami merges with EA to produce a game together

As of now this soccer game is back to the roots. if u played pes 6 or older winning eleven this is time for u to get this u wont be disappointed

AHKCG4DIC74P6POPI2MOLN732JKQ.product_description: Rogue agent Rico Rodriguez journeys to Solis, a huge South American world home of conflict, oppression and extreme weather conditions. Strap into your wingsuit, equip your fully customizable grappling hook, and get ready to bring the thunder! Gold Edition includes: Just Cause 4 – Full game; Neon Racer Pack which includes the following DLC items - Neon Racer Supercar: Blitz around in your very own modified supercar with boost valve on tap. You'll be the fastest thing in Solis; Neon Racer Wingsuit: Race across the skies with this unique neon design; Neon Racer Parachute: Drift over the clouds at speed with this unique neon design; Expansion Pass – Unlock access to each of the three expansions upon their release. More details on the expansion episodes will become available when the game is released; Golden Gear Pack – Contains the coveted Golden Gear Shotgun featuring ricochet tracer secondary fire, plus the Golden Gear Wingsuit and Golden Gear Parachute; Digital Deluxe Content – Fight back against the Black Hand with their own technology! Includes special Black Hand DLC weaponry: Black Hand Stealth Micro Jet: Special aircraft with retractable wings and experimental stealth optic camouflage; Black Hand Prototype Weaponized Wingsuit: The Black Hand’s secret weapon – this prototype wingsuit features boost technology and enhanced missile weaponry.
AHKCG4DIC74P6POPI2MOLN732JKQ.review_text: Game has everything.  Open world.  First person shooting.  Flying around.  One of the most fun things is attaching rockets and balloons to objects or creatures.

AHKCG4DIC74P6POPI2MOLN732JKQ.product_description: The award-winning series returns as Karl Fairburne fights to uncover Project Kraken in 1944 France. The genre-defining authentic sniping, with enhanced kill cam, has never looked or felt better as you fight across immersive maps to stop the Nazi war machine in its tracks. The latest instalment in the award-winning series, Sniper Elite 5 offers unparalleled sniping, tactical third-person combat and an enhanced kill cam. Fight your way across the most immersive maps yet, with many real-world locations captured in stunning detail, and an improved traversal system that lets you explore more of them than ever before. France, 1944 – As part of a covert US Rangers operation to weaken the Atlantikwall fortifications along the coast of Brittany, elite marksman Karl Fairburne makes contact with the French Resistance. Soon they uncover a secret Nazi project that threatens to end the war before the Allies can even invade Europe: Operation Kraken.
AHKCG4DIC74P6POPI2MOLN732JKQ.review_text: I bought this on the day it came out.  While it does have a few bugs (or "glitches"), I still found the game playable and very enjoyable.  I encountered no show-stopping problems.

AG74TXVWXWWULRQGDA4FP44OAAHQ.product_description: Satisfye - ZenGrip Pro Gen 3 OLED, a Switch Grip Compatible with Nintendo Switch - Comfortable & Ergonomic Grip, Joy Con & Switch Control. #1 Switch Accessories Designed for Gamers (Blue)
AG74TXVWXWWULRQGDA4FP44OAAHQ.review_text: Actually has the feeling of a controller. Love it.
predicted_themes: Hardware Build Quality Comfort and Connectivity

AG74TXVWXWWULRQGDA4FP44OAAHQ.product_description: The Turtle Beach Stealth 700 is the latest premium gaming headset for Xbox One, debuting Microsoft’s new Xbox Wireless technology and Windows Sonic surround sound, plus active noise-cancellation and Bluetooth connectivity to the new Turtle Beach Audio Hub app. The Stealth 700 features an all-new modern style and delivers immersive surround sound plus chat audio through powerful 50mm over-ear speakers, and features Turtle Beach’s renowned high-sensitivity mic that can be flipped-up to mute. For comfort, the Stealth 700 dawns the latest iteration of Turtle Beach’s unique ProSpecs glasses friendly design, and has a synthetic leather-wrapped headband and ear-cushions. The Stealth 700 also includes Turtle Beach’s exclusive Superhuman Hearing sound setting, Mic Monitoring, independent game and chat volume control, Dynamic Chat Boost, audio presets including Bass Boost, and a 10-hour rechargeable battery.
AG74TXVWXWWULRQGDA4FP44OAAHQ.review_text:
I bought this to replace my turtle beach elite 800x. This is a slight downgrade, but that's obvious. My only issue though is the wireless connection isn't as good as the 800x, so i do get distortion every now and then.
That said, these are quite a bit cheaper than the 800x's and i didn't have the extra cash to replace them at the time.

AG74TXVWXWWULRQGDA4FP44OAAHQ.product_description: From the makers of the most award-winning headset line in gaming history, the arctis 9x combines the convenience of integrated Xbox wireless with simultaneous Bluetooth audio for true wireless everywhere. With the noise-cancelling clearcast bidirectional Microphone and stunningly detailed arctis sound, the arctis 9x is the premier wireless headset for Xbox gamers. Microphone Frequency Response - 100–10000 HzMicrophone Pattern - BidirectionalMicrophone Sensitivity - -38 dBV/PaMicrophone Impedance - 2200 OhmMicrophone Location - Retractable.
AG74TXVWXWWULRQGDA4FP44OAAHQ.review_text:
Supposedly this is a great headset. Its comfortable sort of.
Major issues though. First, it loses connection to the xbox, alot.
Also the mic was distorted. I could deal with this, but the connection issues is unusable. My turtle beach stealth 700 works way better, and haven't lost connection once. Sad since its half the price of the steel series...
predicted_themes: Hardware Build Quality Comfort and Connectivity

AG74TXVWXWWULRQGDA4FP44OAAHQ.product_description: ENGAGE YOUR PASSION AND FIGHT TO SAVE THE WORLD!A brand new adventure awaits in a fantasy world filled with magic, knights, dragons and mystery in Tales of Zestiria. Two nations fight for supremacy and the fate of the realm lies in the hands of Sorey, an inquisitive young adventurer who takes on the burden of becoming the Shepherd, the one the legends foretold would become the savior of all. Together with Lailah, the Lady of the Lake, who guards the Sacred Blade, and his best friend Mikleo, Sorey soon discovers a powerful force rising in the shadows.
AG74TXVWXWWULRQGDA4FP44OAAHQ.review_text: I played this after Tales of Beseria, so it felt like a downgrade. It was nice getting a different perspective on the world,  but Beseria is soo much better.

AGQXUPHVOU7PHT6SVFMHWG7A3F3Q.product_description: Get the all-digital Xbox Series S – Fortnite & Rocket League Bundle featuring the Midnight Drive Pack, 1,000 V-Bucks, and 1,000 Rocket League credits. With the Midnight Drive Pack for Fortnite, you can customize your avatar with the Dark Skully outfit, Dark Skully Satchel Back Bling, Dark Splitter pickaxe, and 1,000 V-Bucks to spend on favorite items in Fortnite Battle Royale and more. For Rocket League add-on content, you get the standout Purple Masamune Car, Purple Virtual Wave Boost, Purple Zefram Wheels, and 1,000 Rocket League Credits that can be used to build Blueprints, upgrade to Rocket Pass Premium, or purchase most-wanted content from the Rocket League Item Shop. And, since Fortnite and Rocket League are free-to-play with Xbox, you can get right to gaming with friends.
AGQXUPHVOU7PHT6SVFMHWG7A3F3Q.review_text: I can't believe I didn't do this sooner. Xbox is Microsoft so is for us and from us. I been playing playstation since Xbox started charging for online play. 😂.  But now Xbox is mostly free online and backwards compatible, lan, split screens. Yes am coming back to XBOX. And you don't have to completely change but I will definitely play more Xbox specially my old gears of war. Btw ps5 doesn't have many ps5 games yet. So am ok not having one of I have a PS4 and I can play most ps5 games also🤔🤔.

AGQXUPHVOU7PHT6SVFMHWG7A3F3Q.product_description: Become a true Pokemon trainer legend with the HORI Nintendo Switch Split Pad Pro (Pokemon Legends: Arceus)! Enjoy the full-size controller experience in handheld mode featuring full-size analog sticks, a precision D-pad, and large shoulder buttons designed for comfort and accuracy, even during marathon gaming sessions. Advanced features include assignable rear triggers, Turbo functionally, and more. Perfect for RPGS, adventure titles and many other genres. Catch 'em all with the Split Pad Pro! (Does not include Motion Controls, HD Rumble, NFC, or IR camera.) Officially Licensed by Nintendo and The Pokemon Company International.
AGQXUPHVOU7PHT6SVFMHWG7A3F3Q.review_text: Not getting those combos done with the original switch controller, With this you will.
predicted_themes: Hardware Build Quality Comfort and Connectivity

AGQXUPHVOU7PHT6SVFMHWG7A3F3Q.product_description: Ignite your gaming nights on your PS5 console with the DualSense Midnight Black wireless controller. Part of a new line-up of galaxy-themed colors, this sleek design takes inspiration from how we view the wonders of space through the night sky, with subtle shades of black and light gray detailing. Discover a deeper, highly immersive gaming experience** that brings the action to life in the palms of your hands. The DualSense wireless controller offers immersive haptic feedback*, dynamic adaptive triggers* and a built-in microphone, all integrated into an iconic comfortable design. (*Available when feature is supported by game. **Compared to DUALSHOCK 4 wireless controller. Internet and account for PlayStation Network required.)
AGQXUPHVOU7PHT6SVFMHWG7A3F3Q.review_text: I bought this PS5 Controller to play PC games and some how the PS4 controller felt more smoother and comfortable.  So I think of you want an upgrade for PC and have a PS4 already just stay with it.  Unless you don't have either. You should get any of this they are great.  O dislike how the Xbox makes a clicking noise when I pressed the button.  But not PS4 I can be in bed by my spouse and she can't here anything playstation controller are very quiet and very very smooth to the feeling.
predicted_themes: Hardware Build Quality Comfort and Connectivity

AGQXUPHVOU7PHT6SVFMHWG7A3F3Q.product_description: Battlefield 2042 is a first-person shooter that marks the return to the iconic all-out warfare of the franchise. In a near-future world transformed by disorder, adapt and overcome dynamically-changing battlegrounds with the help of your squad and a cutting-edge arsenal.With support for 128 players, Battlefield 2042 brings unprecedented scale on vast battlegrounds across the globe. Players will take on several massive experiences, from updated multiplayer modes like Conquest and Breakthrough to the all-new Hazard Zone. *CONDITIONS AND RESTRICTIONS APPLY. System Requirements: Operating System: Windows 10 Operating System: Windows 10 Steam account required to download and install.
AGQXUPHVOU7PHT6SVFMHWG7A3F3Q.review_text: I couldn't make work on pc it just restarts and stays on loading time. And when it works, it lags like nothing else. I feel like am been force to play on console and pay for ps plus or xbox live. It works fine on ps4. But it just doesn't feel like B4 or B2 or B3. I play once for an hour or two then i was done with it. I recommend you to try once you know it works fine on PC. Is not worth the hassle.

AGBP7INUVYPUQDPHDTHZH6SOTDOA.product_description: Easily control your Xbox One system with the convenient officially licensed Talon Media Remote for Xbox One. Want quick and simple access to all your favorite movies, TV shows, music, and videos, as well as Complete control of channel and volume for your TV? Coordinate your system with the Talon Media Remote and stay in charge right from the comfort of your couch. With motion activated backlit buttons, a soft rubberized texture, and D pad/A, B, X, Y button control, you’ll have everything you need for a Complete Xbox One experience.

AGBP7INUVYPUQDPHDTHZH6SOTDOA.product_description: The DualShock 4 Wireless Controller features familiar controls, and incorporates several innovative features to usher in a new era of interactive experiences. Its definitive analog sticks and trigger buttons have been improved for greater feel and sensitivity. A multi touch, clickable touch pad expands gameplay possibilities, while the incorporated light bar in conjunction with the PlayStation Camera allows for easy player identification and screen adjustment when playing with friends in the same room. The addition of the Share button makes utilizing the social capabilities of the PlayStation 4 as easy as the push of a button. The DualShock 4 Wireless Controller is more than a controller; it's your physical connection to a new era of gaming.

AGBP7INUVYPUQDPHDTHZH6SOTDOA.product_description: The Ultimate Portable is a stylish handheld video game player loaded with 80 built-in 16-bit SEGA arcade games. A large 3.2 display makes for convenient handheld play. With the built-in rechargeable battery you can have hours of fun without having to buy expensive batteries.
